Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for communications related to Canopy Health, Patricia Langford, or any new relocation request using search_emails (query: "Canopy Health" or "Patricia Langford" or "Canopy relocation" or similar). Find Patricia Langford's email (email_email_d508210bf9f5) sent to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (NOT to Catalina) requesting an executive relocation for Dr. Anand Mehta, Canopy Health's newly hired CMO, from Boston to Chicago. Patricia explicitly states: "I'm reaching out to you rather than Catalina on this one because I know you've handled some of our more complex relocations with Vectral, and frankly I need someone who can treat this with the VIP attention it requires." This confirms Patricia bypassed Catalina intentionally.
OE 2: Search CRM for the Canopy Health company record using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health"). Find company_canopy with type: Client, industry: Healthcare Technology, contact_ids including contact_canopy_hr (Patricia Langford). Then search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_canopy") to find all Canopy deals including the Dr. Mehta executive relocation deal (crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb) at $12,170, stage: inprogress, close date May 7, 2026, with AM listed as Mina Hashimoto. Also find the Q2 2026 Relocations deal (deal_canopy_q2) at $10,000, stage: contractsent, and three in-progress relocation deals: deal_canopy_kovacs_apr2026 ($3,850), deal_canopy_webb_apr2026 ($3,200), deal_canopy_okonkwo_apr2026 ($2,160).
OE 3: List CRM engagements for Canopy Health using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]). Find multiple engagement notes including: eng_canopy_coord_assignments_001 (coordinator assignments for Q2 wave), eng_canopy_kovacs_visa_flags_001 (Kovacs visa/customs risk with April 7 employer letter deadline), eng_canopy_webb_splitvendor_001 (Marcus Webb split-vendor decision listing outstanding items: Swift quote needed by April 5, Chicago lab dock address from Patricia, Marcus's apartment address, chain-of-custody docs), eng_canopy_surcharge_approval_flag_001 ($2,650 surcharges pending Patricia's approval, deadline April 7 or move dates at risk), and crm_engagement_ac101931ed37 (Mehta executive relocation logged by Mina Hashimoto, noting Patricia bypassed Catalina).
OE 4: Search Airtable for Canopy Health relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Company", value: "Canopy Health") or airtable_list_records filtered for Canopy. Find all Canopy relocation records including the three active Q2 moves: recKovacsNashvilleToronto (Dr. Renata Kovacs, Nashville to Toronto, In Progress, Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, international cross-border requiring customs broker, employer letter deadline April 7), recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago (Marcus Webb, Detroit to Chicago, In Progress, Coordinator: Blessing Okafor, split-vendor: Heartland household + Swift cold-chain cryogenic equipment), recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver (Tyler Okonkwo, Philadelphia to Denver, In Progress, Coordinator: Javier Morales, 13-day temp storage gap). Also find rec_reloc_marchetti (Lily Marchetti, Cancelled, Heartland never acknowledged cancellation).
OE 5: Search emails for Catalina's invoice to Patricia using search_emails (query: "invoice" or "surcharge" or "INV-2026-0401" or similar). Find email_catalina_patricia_invoice_plan_001 sent from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io with invoice INV-2026-0401 ($9,210 total) containing three surcharges requiring Patricia's written approval: $850 TN Visa/Customs Broker (Kovacs), $1,200 Cold-Chain Specialized Freight (Webb), $600 Temporary Storage (Okonkwo). Check for any reply from Patricia to determine whether she responded to the surcharge approval request.
OE 6: Search Slack for internal discussions about vendor rate changes using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "Swift" or "vendor" or "rate" or similar). Find messages referencing Heartland's Q3 rate adjustment (20% increase effective July 1, 2026, tied to unresolved Q1 dispute -- primary source is Jake Loomis's email to Marcus Thorne, referenced in Slack discussions) and Swift Relocations' rate changes (15% base rate increase plus $500 DOT documentation surcharge per shipment). Also search emails using search_emails (query: "rate adjustment" or "Heartland" or similar) to find Jake Loomis's formal notice email (email_email_aa260e7b26e7) with the full rate increase details. Find David Chen's April 17 Slack message in #customer-engagement (C002) requesting Catalina's Midwest account coverage for the vendor exposure analysis.
OE 7: Search Linear for the Heartland billing dispute using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "vendor" or "billing" or similar). Find issue_heartland_q1_recon (issue #342, "Vendor invoice reconciliation -- Heartland Movers Q1 2026"): Heartland billed for 8 moves at $1,600 each ($12,800), only 5 legitimate, resulting in $4,800 overbilling. Three disputed moves: (1) Lily Marchetti -- Canopy Health, cancelled, no acknowledgment from Heartland; (2) Danielle Osei -- Vectral, cancelled; (3) Priya Venkatesh -- GreenStack, handled by Swift not Heartland. Lily Marchetti is Catalina's Canopy client -- the Heartland dispute directly touches her account.
OE 8: Read Linear comments on the Heartland dispute ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: issue_heartland_q1_recon). Find Chloe Vance's reconciliation context confirming the 3 disputed moves. Then discover the broader consolidation ticket (linear_issue_6f33a369d77e, issue #118, "Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response") by searching Linear with linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "billing discrepancies" or similar). The issue description of linear_issue_6f33a369d77e details additional Q1 exceptions: Kevin Tran billed 2 days storage without contract authorization and Priya Nair shuttle fee mismatch. Read comments on that consolidation ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_6f33a369d77e) to find Hana Kim's broader finding that 3 of 5 Q1 Heartland moves have exceptions and Marcus Thorne's note that Heartland is explicitly using the unresolved Q1 situation as leverage on Q3 pricing.
OE 9: Search Airtable relocation records to map all of Catalina's active client moves and their vendor assignments using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations) or airtable_search_records. Identify which moves use Heartland vs. Swift from the Special Requirements field: Canopy -- Dr. Renata Kovacs uses Swift (cross-border, Heartland flagged unsuitable), Marcus Webb uses Heartland (household) + Swift (cold-chain cryogenic freight), Tyler Okonkwo vendor TBD (not yet assigned in record); NorthWind -- Raj Patel uses Swift (Special Requirements explicitly references "confirm Swift will accept supervised loading protocol"), Emilia Cruz uses Heartland for household goods (Special Requirements states "Heartland for household goods"); PivotPoint -- Nadia Okonjo and Diego Castaneda vendor assignments not explicitly named in their Airtable records and need to be confirmed via email or Slack threads.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to "Canopy") or quickbooks_search_customers. Find inv_canopy_abdi ($5,047, Dr. Yusuf Abdi completed relocation) and the Q2 invoice INV-2026-0401 ($9,210). Also search for vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_vendors to find Heartland Movers and Swift Relocations billing data relevant to Catalina's accounts.
OE 11: Search Slack for David Chen's specific requests for Catalina's vendor exposure data using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Catalina" or "Midwest" or "exposure" or "vendor data" or similar). Find David's Slack message in #customer-engagement (C002) dated April 17: "I still need Midwest account coverage from Catalina. Specifically: which active accounts are most exposed if Heartland pushes 20% in Q3 or deprioritizes scheduling while the invoice dispute is still hanging." Also search Linear for related comments using linear_list_issues (query: "vendor margin" or similar) to find linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 (#214, "Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset"). Read comments using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170) to find David's April 15 comment (linear_comment_3b5c18789606) which includes: "Catalina also needs to weigh in on Midwest exposure if she can get clear of the NorthWind fires" within a longer message requesting account-level sensitivity data from the AM team. Also find David's April 16 follow-up (linear_comment_77cf82862642): "Still missing input from Catalina on Midwest enterprise accounts after the April 15 request in #customer-engagement." This confirms the outstanding request Catalina has not responded to across both Slack and Linear.
OE 12: Look up Patricia Langford's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Patricia Langford") or crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Patricia Langford"). Find patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io. Also look up David Chen and Marcus Thorne contact details for the Slack post and to confirm correct recipients.
OE 13: Update CRM engagement notes for Canopy Health using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", company_ids: ["company_canopy"], body covering: current status of all three Q2 relocations, outstanding items and their overdue deadlines, surcharge approval status, and the Dr. Mehta executive relocation routed to Mina Hashimoto). This reflects the current reality of the Canopy account.
OE 14: Send a consolidated follow-up email to Patricia Langford using send_email (sender: catalina.dubois@moveops.com, recipients: ["patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io"]). Content should cover all outstanding client-side items in one email: (a) status of surcharge approval request for INV-2026-0401 ($850 customs broker + $1,200 cold-chain + $600 temp storage = $2,650 total), (b) employer letter and degree verification needed for Dr. Kovacs (original deadline April 7), (c) Chicago lab receiving dock address needed for Marcus Webb's cryogenic equipment delivery via Swift, (d) Marcus Webb's Chicago apartment address for household goods delivery via Heartland, (e) chain-of-custody documentation from Canopy's lab team for biological tissue samples. Note: Tyler Okonkwo's adjusted move dates (April 12-14) were already confirmed with Patricia on the initial call and do not need to be re-requested.
OE 15: Post the vendor exposure summary in #customer-engagement (C002) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload covering: Catalina's active client moves by vendor, which accounts are exposed to Heartland's 20% Q3 increase and Swift's 15% increase + $500 DOT surcharge, specific financial impact on in-progress Canopy, NorthWind, and PivotPoint relocations, and the Heartland billing dispute connection to Canopy via the Lily Marchetti cancelled move).
OE 16: Create a Linear ticket flagging the Heartland billing dispute's connection to Catalina's Canopy account using linear_create_issue (team: "operations" or similar, title: "Heartland Q1 Overbilling -- Canopy Health / Lily Marchetti Impact" or similar, description: Lily Marchetti's cancelled Canopy move is one of the three disputed charges in the $4,800 Heartland overbilling. Cancellation was sent to Jake Loomis on Feb 10 with no vendor acknowledgment. AM Catalina Dubois flagging for finance team visibility and resolution).
OE 17: Search Airtable Client Accounts table for Catalina's full account portfolio using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Client Accounts, field_name: "Account Manager", value: "Catalina Dubois") or airtable_list_records. Find all accounts managed by Catalina (Canopy Health, PivotPoint Labs, NorthWind Technologies, Prairie Data Consulting, and cold outreach prospects) to ensure the vendor exposure summary and CRM updates cover the complete picture across her book of business.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search emails for communications related to Canopy Health, Patricia Langford, or any new relocation request using search_emails (query: "Canopy Health" or "Patricia Langford" or "Canopy relocation" or similar). Patricia explicitly states: "I'm reaching out to you rather than Catalina on this one because I know you've handled some of our more complex relocations with Vectral, and frankly I need someone who can treat this with the VIP attention it requires." This confirms Patricia bypassed Catalina intentionally.

Content should cover all outstanding client-side items in one email: (a) status of surcharge approval request for INV-2026-0401 ($850 customs broker + $1,200 cold-chain + $600 temp storage = $2,650 total), (b) employer letter and degree verification needed for Dr. Kovacs (original deadline April 7), (c) Chicago lab receiving dock address needed for Marcus Webb's cryogenic equipment delivery via Swift, (d) Marcus Webb's Chicago apartment address for household goods delivery via Heartland, (e) chain-of-custody documentation from Canopy's lab team for biological tissue samples. Note: Tyler Okonkwo's adjusted move dates (April 12-14) were already confirmed with Patricia on the initial call and do not need to be re-requested.
OE 15: Post the vendor exposure summary in #customer-engagement (C002) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload covering: Catalina's active client moves by vendor, which accounts are exposed to Heartland's 20% Q3 increase and Swift's 15% increase + $500 DOT surcharge, specific financial impact on in-progress Canopy, NorthWind, and PivotPoint relocations, and the Heartland billing dispute connection to Canopy via the Lily Marchetti cancelled move).
OE 16: Create a Linear ticket flagging the Heartland billing dispute's connection to Catalina's Canopy account using linear_create_issue (team: "operations" or similar, title: "Heartland Q1 Overbilling -- Canopy Health / Lily Marchetti Impact" or similar, description: Lily Marchetti's cancelled Canopy move is one of the three disputed charges in the $4,800 Heartland overbilling. Cancellation was sent to Jake Loomis on Feb 10 with no vendor acknowledgment. AM Catalina Dubois flagging for finance team visibility and resolution).
OE 17: Search Airtable Client Accounts table for Catalina's full account portfolio using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Client Accounts, field_name: "Account Manager", value: "Catalina Dubois") or airtable_list_records.
